failedAssertions.reason: Two-agent CLI run exited 0 but produced only two_agent_metrics.json in the specified output directory, so slop-code eval failed (missing environment.yaml) and metric schema parity with baseline could not be established.
Checkpoint directory listings differ between baseline and two-agent outputs for dag_execution (baseline has checkpoint_1, two-agent has none).
Two-agent run exited 0 but produced only two_agent_metrics.json; no eval-compatible run structure was created and `slop-code eval` failed (`environment.yaml` missing).
Budget split is printed as 70/30, but telemetry has zero implementer/reviewer tokens and zero costs for all checkpoints, so enforcement is not reflected in per-checkpoint token/cost tracking.
The metrics JSON contains all six required keys, but all values are default zeros across checkpoints, indicating metrics were not actually captured from checkpoint execution.
With budget=0.01, run should abort non-zero on budget overrun and save partial results, but run completed with exit 0, `budget_exceeded=false`, and all checkpoints marked complete.
Canary started with the default $0.50 budget and passed preflight, but failed during checkpoint_1 implementer execution (exit code 1), so it did not complete an eval-compatible run under canary mode.

Docker-unavailable failure was descriptive and component-tagged ([Docker]), but the invalid API key scenario was not surfaced as an API/authentication diagnostic and instead failed later as a generic implementer exit.
Canary output directory was not compatible with `slop-code eval`; eval failed with missing `environment.yaml`, and required run artifacts/checkpoint outputs were absent.
`--budget-split 100` is rejected by argument validation (`must be in range 1-99`) and does not run implementer-only mode.

Concurrent runs used distinct output directories and run IDs, but no Docker containers were created and both runs recorded zero cost, so full isolation contract (including independent containers and meaningful per-run cost tracking) was not met.

blockedAssertions.blockedBy: Dolt connection succeeded but experiments table is absent in scbench, so row insertion validation for both modes cannot be exercised through the user CLI surface.
Budget table is absent in scbench, so spent/remaining before-after budget delta checks cannot be measured.
Insufficient-budget gate path cannot be validated because pipeline budget check fails immediately with table-not-found for budget instead of evaluating remaining < estimated.
Could not compare per-checkpoint eval metrics to stored values because querying experiments metrics failed with table-not-found.
Could not validate partial-results persistence for budget-aborted runs because experiments table query failed with table-not-found.
Could not validate 27-column experiments schema because DESCRIBE experiments failed with table-not-found.
Could not validate manipulation_check propagation/filter behavior because grouped and filtered experiments queries failed with table-not-found.
Could not validate baseline-vs-two-agent comparable rows and delta fields because experiments comparison query failed with table-not-found.
Reviewer-phase lines appear in logs, but no reviewer artifacts were produced and no injected reviewer context could be observed in subsequent implementer inputs through user-surface artifacts.

frictions.description: Non-canary runner flow reports phase completion even when underlying `python -m slop_code run` subprocess invocation fails, which can yield apparently successful runs with zero metrics and no run artifacts.
frictions.resolution: Captured evidence and treated impacted assertions as fail/blocked.

frictions.description: Canary failure output for implementer errors only reports the phase exit code and omits underlying stderr details, which made root-cause diagnosis for invalid-key behavior indirect.
Baseline slop-code run for dag_execution errored at checkpoint_1 but still wrote eval artifacts for one checkpoint.
frictions.resolution: Used the generated output directory and re-ran slop-code eval --overwrite to collect deterministic evidence.

blockers.description: The subprocess form used in validation (`python -m slop_code run`) fails in this environment (`No module named slop_code.__main__`), preventing checkpoint artifacts, eval-compatible outputs, and reviewer artifact generation.
blockers.quickFixAttempted: Validated behavior repeatedly across normal, low-budget, concurrent, and resume flows; captured direct CLI error evidence.

blockers.description: Required Dolt research tables experiments and budget are missing from the scbench database in this environment.
blockers.quickFixAttempted: Verified pymysql import and Dolt connectivity, listed tables with SHOW TABLES, and attempted direct SELECT queries on both tables; each query returned table-not-found (1146).

blockers.description: The Dolt scbench database is reachable but contains only Gas Town tracking tables; experiments is missing, blocking all assigned metrics/storage assertions.
blockers.quickFixAttempted: Verified DB connectivity and enumerated tables, then executed assertion-specific SQL; each experiments query returned ProgrammingError 1146 table-not-found.

appliedUpdates.description: Updated known frictions/blockers with latest round findings (subprocess invocation, canary diagnostics, budget-split boundary, and Dolt table prerequisites).
